verses.text: In that day the LORD shall punish 'leviathan the piercing serpent,' slaying the dragon in the sea. Evil's great powers are crushed. God will at last destroy the serpent and all his works.

Now sing of the LORD's vineyard: 'I the LORD do keep it; I will water it every moment... lest any hurt it.' His care is constant. Unlike the ruined vineyard of chapter 5, this one He tends with unfailing love.

Now sing of the LORD's vineyard: 'I the LORD do keep it; I will water it every moment... lest any hurt it.' His care is constant. Unlike the ruined vineyard of chapter 5, this one He tends with unfailing love.

Now sing of the LORD's vineyard: 'I the LORD do keep it; I will water it every moment... lest any hurt it.' His care is constant. Unlike the ruined vineyard of chapter 5, this one He tends with unfailing love.

He invites the enemy to 'take hold of my strength' and make peace; then Israel shall blossom and fill the world with fruit. Reconciliation is offered. Those who lay down their arms and cling to God become fruitful.

He invites the enemy to 'take hold of my strength' and make peace; then Israel shall blossom and fill the world with fruit. Reconciliation is offered. Those who lay down their arms and cling to God become fruitful.
By chastening, the iniquity of Jacob is purged, when he casts away his idols and altars. Affliction cleanses. God's discipline aims not to destroy but to purify His people from sin.
By chastening, the iniquity of Jacob is purged, when he casts away his idols and altars. Affliction cleanses. God's discipline aims not to destroy but to purify His people from sin.
By chastening, the iniquity of Jacob is purged, when he casts away his idols and altars. Affliction cleanses. God's discipline aims not to destroy but to purify His people from sin.

The fortified city is left desolate, for they are 'a people of no understanding,' so their Maker shows them no favour. Senseless rebellion forfeits mercy. To refuse understanding is to refuse God's grace.

The fortified city is left desolate, for they are 'a people of no understanding,' so their Maker shows them no favour. Senseless rebellion forfeits mercy. To refuse understanding is to refuse God's grace.

In that day God shall gather His people 'one by one,' and the great trumpet shall summon the outcasts to worship in Jerusalem. None is overlooked. God carefully gathers each of His scattered ones home.
